revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a game-development pixel-art animated sprite sheet.

Use the attached reference image as the exact character identity reference. Treat this as re-posing the same approved character, not redesigning them. Match the reference character's proportions, slim-but-sturdy body width, head-to-body ratio, hair shape, outfit palette, outline style, pixel density, and bright 16-bit-plus pixel-art rendering. Keep the same character identity and exact same scale in all frames.

Canvas and layout:
- Exact image size: 1024x512 px.
- Grid arrangement: 4 columns x 2 rows, 8 frames total.
- Each frame cell is 256x256 px.
- TOP row = frames 1, 2, 3, 4 left to right.
- BOTTOM row = frames 5, 6, 7, 8 left to right.
- Do not draw grid lines, separator lines, frame borders, frame numbers, labels, text, signatures, logos, or watermarks.
- Fill all empty space with solid #FF00FF chroma-key magenta, perfectly flat, no gradients, no texture.
- Leave clear solid magenta margin between the figure/sword and every cell edge.
- Every frame must stay fully inside its own 256x256 cell; no body part, sword piece, or pixel fragment may cross a cell boundary.
- Exactly one character per cell.
- Feet on the same ground line in every cell.
- Character body height about 70 percent of the cell height so the raised sword fits inside the cell with margin.

Style:
- Crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with readable pixel clusters.
- No antialiasing blur, no painterly softness, no vector smoothness.
- Consistent bright cheerful palette.
- Dark one-pixel outline where useful.
- Top-left light source.
- Clean solid shapes only.
- No detached fragments, no isolated pixel islands, no dust specks, no leftover residue.

Character:
- Original bright mythical-Japan action RPG hero named Mito.
- Androgynous 15-year-old wanderer.
- Facing UP in all frames, back view, attacking away from the viewer.
- Show the back of the head with short ink-black hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, plus vermilion braided-cord hair ornament.
- No face visible in any frame; no eyes visible because this is a back/up-facing view.
- Outfit: white tube-sleeved top, short indigo-blue haori jacket, small vermilion braided-cord knots on sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, vermilion braided cord belt-sash with small tassel, necklace with three pale-green magatama beads only if visible from the pose, and a sheathed short ritual sword at left hip in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers.
- Weapon during attack: short ritual sword with straight bright blade, WHITE hilt wrapped with thin sacred rope and small white paper streamers.
- The sword must read as a festival ritual implement, not a katana.
- NOT samurai, NOT ninja: no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no daisho pair.

Identity and scale lock:
- Every frame depicts the exact same character at the exact same scale.
- Keep head size, head-to-body ratio, body width, limb thickness, total height, and pixel density identical in all frames.
- Re-pose the character only; do not redraw, restyle, resize, or change the silhouette proportions.
- The standing body height in pixels must match across all upright frames.
- Do not make the character wider or stockier than the reference.

Animation: 8-frame sword slash facing UP, one continuous motion, no effects. Draw only the solid body and solid sword.
Frame 1: WINDUP start. Hilt gripped, body coils, feet planted on the shared ground line, back view.
Frame 2: WINDUP peak. Blade raised high above the head, clearly visible over the shoulders, still fully inside the cell.
Frame 3: STRIKE. Blade sweeping up-and-away in front of the character, partly hidden by the body from the back/up-facing view.
Frame 4: STRIKE end. Arms fully extended upward-forward, decisive key pose, sword fully contained.
Frame 5: FOLLOW-THROUGH. Swing decelerates, body still facing up/back view.
Frame 6: FOLLOW-THROUGH settles. Weight returns toward center, same scale and ground line.
Frame 7: RECOVERY toward ready guard. Blade returns closer to the side.
Frame 8: Ready guard stance. Blade at the side, pose loops cleanly into frame 1.

Strict exclusions:
- No motion trail, no swoosh, no afterimage, no energy effect, no glow, no particles, no impact effects.
- No detached fragments, no floating paper bits outside the connected character/weapon shapes.
- No bird, no companion creature, no extra objects, no UI.
- No modern objects, no gun.
- No dark gloomy palette or horror mood.
- No letter emblems.
- Avoid any likeness of Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos, or protected designs.

Output must be a single clean 1024x512 PNG sprite sheet on solid #FF00FF background.
